title: Aegis Agent Manifest
description: The signed contract every Aegis agent (first-party or third-party add-on) must satisfy to run on the platform. It is the artifact CI validates and the contract the MCP authorization gateway enforces. See docs/04-AGENT-ONBOARDING-STANDARD.md.

kind.description: Resource kind. Always 'Agent' for an agent manifest.

metadata.description: Identity, ownership, compliance packs, and data classification for the agent.

id.description: Unique agent id (kebab-case). Used in audit lineage and as a registry key.

description.description: Short description of what the agent does and its decision posture.

owner.description: Owning department / cost-allocation + chargeback owner. Maps to the 'dept' cost-allocation tag.

team.description: Optional owning team within the department (maps to the 'team' cost-allocation tag).

packs.description: Compliance overlay packs that must be active in the target environment for this agent to deploy. Deploy fails if a required pack is not active.

classification.description: Data classes this agent is permitted to touch. Masking is enforced for every declared class and fails closed.

blast_radius.description: Declared impact level of the agent's actions. Drives review depth and human-gate strictness.

grants.description: Least-privilege grants. At runtime the gateway intersects these with the acting human's entitlement (permitted = grant ∩ entitlement). Anything not declared is denied.

tools.description: Tools / MCP servers the agent may reach. Each must be registered in the gateway tool registry.

scope.description: Access scope granted for this tool.

data_classes.description: Optional per-tool restriction of which declared data classes may flow through this tool. Must be a subset of metadata.classification.

consequential.description: Consequential actions (issue/adjudicate/release/award/transfer) that are WITHHELD from the agent and only reachable via the human gate. CI proves these are absent from the agent's executable grants.

items.description: Registered tool id of a consequential action.

grounding.description: RAG grounding configuration. A grounding source is required; no ungrounded free-generation against systems of record.

knowledge_base.description: Required Bedrock Knowledge Base id (or list) the agent grounds against.

grounding_threshold.description: Minimum Bedrock contextual-grounding score required to return a response. Bedrock supports 0–0.99; higher is stricter.

relevance_threshold.description: Optional minimum contextual-relevance score (0–0.99) for retrieved passages.

budget.description: Token budget and chargeback configuration. No unbounded spend; all spend is attributable. See docs/05-FINOPS-TOKEN-BUDGETS-CHARGEBACK.md.

monthly_token_cap.description: Hard cap on monthly input+output tokens. The gateway rejects or throttles calls that would breach the cap (fail-closed on budget).

inference_profile.description: Bedrock application inference profile name/alias used for cost attribution (the profile ARN is used in place of the model id at call time).

cap_behavior.description: Behavior when the cap is reached. 'hard' denies; 'soft' warns and continues.

alert_thresholds.description: Fractions of the monthly cap at which alerts fire to the owner (e.g. 0.6, 0.85, 1.0). Ascending, unique.

evals.description: Required eval suite and minimum pass rate. Must pass before promotion and is re-run on model/prompt change.

suite.description: Path to the eval suite (accuracy, refusal, fairness/four-fifths, prompt-injection resistance, accessibility for user-facing output).

min_pass_rate.description: Minimum fraction of eval cases that must pass to promote.

categories.description: Optional list of eval categories this suite covers.

human_gate.description: Human-in-the-loop gate for consequential actions. Approval tokens are bound to the exact tool+arguments, single-use, separation-of-duties, and expiring.

mode.description: Mechanism used to suspend execution and wait for human approval.

separation_of_duties.description: If true, the approver must differ from the requestor (enforced at approval time).

approval_ttl_seconds.description: Time-to-live for an approval token before it expires.

esignature_grade.description: If true, approvals are attributable/non-repudiable to 21 CFR Part 11 electronic-signature grade (required by the healthcare-lifesciences GxP pack).

signing.description: Publisher signature. The gateway verifies the signature at load; unsigned or untrusted manifests are rejected.

publisher.description: Known publisher identity (department or ISV) trusted to sign manifests.

signature.description: Detached signature over the manifest (cosign / JWS).
